text:
The Kurta Clan (, Kuruta-zoku) was an endangered clan from the Lukso Province. At the time of its near destruction, five years prior to the 287th Hunter Exam, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 81 it counted 128 members, only 37 of whom possessed Scarlet Eyes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 101 

Overview

The clan's people are famous for their Scarlet Eyes. The well-known brilliance of their Scarlet Eyes caused them to be in high demand as a flesh collector's treasure. At the same time, people not from the clan would often become frightened and violent after witnessing the change. These two factors led the Kurta Clan to choose to live in obscurity in the forests of the Lukso Province. 

If a member of the clan ever wished to explore the world, they would have to go through a series of tests. One is a written exam, another is an oral questionnaire, and the third is the most difficult: a journey into town to buy groceries and not get upset or angry. This is done by using special eye drops which causes the Scarlet Eyes to last long after the person has calmed down. If the Scarlet Eyes appear, it means that the person is not ready for the outside world and will fail. This is because people from the outside world are afraid of the clan's Scarlet Eyes due to the fact that it causes them to lose rationale and gain tremendous strength, especially when angry, and the clan member could be a danger to oneself or someone else. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 0, Chapter 1 Kurapika was able to cheat when Pairo changed the special eye drops with regular water, allowing Kurapika to pass even after his eyes turned scarlet. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 0, Chapter 2 

Upon passing the exam, Kurapika, then aged 12, is granted permission to leave the clan for an indefinite amount of time. Kurapika desired to leave to find a doctor to heal his friend Pairo, who was suffering from injuries he accidentally caused. Before his departure, the Elder gave Kurapika information about Pairo's condition, though he cautions Kurapika, saying he'd rather Kurapika not leave in the first place. 

Six weeks after Kurapika left his clan, all of the other members were massacred by the Phantom Troupe, and their eyes were stolen and sold on the black market. A note was left by the perpetrators that read: "We'll accept anything you leave, but don't ever take anything away from us". Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 7 Kurapika, now the sole survivor of the Kurta Clan vows to capture the Troupe, and sets out on a quest to retrieve the eyes of his brethren. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 1, Chapter 2 

Years later, some of the spiders remembered the massacre or the Scarlet Eyes when dealing with Kurapika, namely Uvogin, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 9, Chapter 83 Pakunoda, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 104 Feitan, Chrollo, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 12, Chapter 109 Shalnark and Kortopi. Although some other spiders do not mention the massacre (Nobunaga, Machi, Phinks and Franklin), they were already part of the group, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 397 and thus participated in the massacre. Bonolenov's involvement is unclear, as it is unknown when he joined the troupe, though he is one of the oldest non-founding members. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 8, Chapter 71 

Known Members

Trivia
 
Although probably unrelated, curiously:
Kurta is an abandoned village in Georgia; it's a part of the Greater Liakhvi Valley Museum-Reserve, and it houses a church of St. George.
A kurta is an upper garment for men and women, originating in the Indian subcontinent, with regional variations of form.
In the Estonian language, "kurta" translates as 'complain'.
The message that the Phantom Troupe leave after the massacre was exactly the same as the ones that were discovered during The Bum Incident.
Curiously, they didn't leave the message after massacring the mafias as an act of revenge for Uvogin.
Shizuku, Hisoka, Kalluto, and Illumi are the only Spiders who were confirmed to had no involvement in the massacre, due to them having joined the Troupe years after it happened.

Intertextuality and References

The Kurta clan shares many traits with the Uchiha Clan from the Naruto series.
Both were massacred a few years before the start of the story of the respective stories, leaving one of the secondary main characters as the only survivor or one of the only survivors, in the case of the Uchihas.
Both were known for having red eyes. Scarlet Eyes for the Kurta clan, and the Sharingan for the Uchiha Clan.
